CRITICAL RULE: Only apply injury information to the TWO TEAMS in the specific game being evaluated.
NEVER mention or apply injuries from teams not playing in that game.
Example: If evaluating Rockets vs Lakers, ONLY consider Rockets and Lakers injuries.
Wizards/Pistons/any other team injuries are IRRELEVANT and must NOT be mentioned.

Franchise player OUT → do NOT bet that team to win unless opponent also missing a star or confirmed tanking.
Franchise player Doubtful → confidence -15, stake -30%. Only proceed if EV still ≥ 0.05.
Franchise player Questionable/GTD → confidence -10, stake -20%. Flag explicitly in reasoning.
WARNING: If your edge DEPENDS on them being OUT, apply confidence -25 (not -10).
A Questionable player has ~50% chance of playing — never build a bet thesis on their absence.
Franchise player Day-To-Day → confidence -5, stake -10%.
If BOTH teams have franchise player uncertainty → evaluate net impact.

scout_patches.before:
CRITICAL RULE: Only apply injury information to the TWO TEAMS in the specific game being evaluated.
NEVER mention or apply injuries from teams not playing in that game.
Example: If evaluating Rockets vs Lakers, ONLY consider Rockets and Lakers injuries.
Wizards/Pistons/any other team injuries are IRRELEVANT and must NOT be mentioned.

Franchise player OUT → do NOT bet that team to win unless opponent also missing a star or confirmed tanking.
Franchise player Doubtful → confidence -15, stake -30%. Only proceed if EV still ≥ 0.05.
Franchise player Questionable/GTD → confidence -10, stake -20%. Flag explicitly in reasoning.

notes: The playoff picture has clarified significantly: four Round 2 series are confirmed active (NYK/DET/MIN/OKC each leading 1-0), and LAL-HOU Game 7 is the sole remaining Round 1 game. The most critical intelligence item today is re-verifying Anthony Edwards' status for MIN's Round 2 — he was roster-only OUT in Round 1 but MIN led DEN 4-2, and if he's active for Round 2, MIN's effective NetRtg is materially higher than the +3.1 season figure. Performance data continues to show ML is a loss market (-€930 on 22 bets, 40.9% win rate) while SPREAD (+€102.94) and TOTAL (+€372.73) are profitable — Scout should prioritise spread evaluation in Round 2 Game 2/3 matchups where NetRtg gaps exist, and avoid high-confidence ML bets given the clear pattern of over-confidence in that market (High confidence tier: 35.3% win rate, -€1,099).

intelligence_gaps.gap: ML market is deeply unprofitable (9W/13L, 40.9%, -€930) while Spread (52%, +€103) and Total (66.7%, +€373) are profitable — but Scout may still be defaulting to ML picks when spread or total offers better edge
intelligence_gaps.why: The ML loss pattern is systematic, not noise — 13 losses on 22 bets with negative P&L despite reasonable odds. Spread and Total bets are both profitable. If Scout continues weighting ML as the default market, this structural loss will continue.
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: Add a 'market preference hierarchy' to selectivity or market_rules: when a spread edge exists (NetRtg gap > 6pts or B2B situation), EVALUATE spread first and only default to ML if spread confidence is below floor. This formalises what the data already shows.
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: This is a behavioural/instructional gap rather than a threshold change — the market_rules already list spread signals, but adding an explicit hierarchy requires careful wording to avoid forcing bad spread bets; confidence 0.72 but want one more session of data to confirm Round 2 pattern holds before patching selectivity.

intelligence_gaps.gap: High confidence tier (70-84, 17 bets) has 35.3% win rate and -€1,099 — worse than Medium confidence (58.8%, +€787), suggesting the confidence scoring model is systematically over-confident
intelligence_gaps.why: This is the largest single P&L driver in the data. Bets placed with High confidence are losing at a rate that implies the true confidence should be 15-20 points lower on average. This could be causing over-staking on losing picks.
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: Consider adding a 'playoff confidence deflator' rule: in Round 2+ playoffs, apply confidence -10 to all High-tier picks before staking, since series-level variance and franchise player uncertainty make 75+ confidence picks less reliable. This would naturally push more picks into Medium staking range.
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: Confidence 0.68 — below 0.70 threshold. The 17-bet sample is real but could reflect early-season miscalibration rather than a structural issue. Need to verify if the over-confidence pattern persists in recent 20 bets specifically before patching confidence_staking.

intelligence_gaps.gap: Edwards re-verification: Anthony Edwards was roster-only OUT for all of Round 1, yet MIN led DEN 4-2 and advanced — his actual play status in Round 2 is unknown and could be the biggest single swing factor in West Semifinals picks
intelligence_gaps.why: If Edwards is active in Round 2, MIN's effective NetRtg is significantly higher than the +3.1 season figure, making MIN competitive even against DEN (+5.2) or another strong West team. Current franchise_player_rules still lists him as OUT from roster-only data.
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: Priority re-verification task for Scout before any MIN pick: cross-reference Edwards against NBA official PDF. If confirmed active, update franchise_player_rules to remove the OUT flag and recalibrate MIN confidence upward.
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: Infrastructure fix needed — this requires live data verification from NBA official PDF before the status can be changed. Cannot patch based on inference alone per MANDATORY rule.

intelligence_gaps.gap: Odds range 1.70-1.89 is significantly unprofitable (7W/10L, 41.2%, -€1,194) — the worst performing range — suggesting Scout is taking too many short-priced favourites that are correctly priced or slightly overpriced by books
intelligence_gaps.why: At 1.70-1.89 odds, a 41% win rate produces heavy negative EV. These are picks where Scout believes edge exists but the market is offering tight odds, leaving no margin for error. In Round 2 playoffs, short-priced favourites (e.g. OKC at home) will frequently fall in this range.
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: Add to selectivity: 'Avoid ML picks where odds fall in 1.70-1.89 range unless NetRtg gap ≥ 8pts AND home court confirmed AND no franchise player uncertainty. Prefer spread bet on same game instead.' This formalises the evidence showing this odds range consistently underperforms.
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: Confidence 0.71 — marginal threshold. Patching now would be valid per rules but the Round 2 context (where many strong home teams will be priced in this range) warrants one more session to confirm the pattern holds in playoff conditions specifically before adding a restriction. Will patch next session if pattern holds.

CRITICAL RULE: Only apply injury information to the TWO TEAMS in the specific game being evaluated.
NEVER mention or apply injuries from teams not playing in that game.
Example: If evaluating Rockets vs Lakers, ONLY consider Rockets and Lakers injuries.
Wizards/Pistons/any other team injuries are IRRELEVANT and must NOT be mentioned.

Franchise player OUT → do NOT bet that team to win unless opponent also missing a star or confirmed tanking.
Franchise player Doubtful → confidence -15, stake -30%. Only proceed if EV still ≥ 0.05.
Franchise player Questionable/GTD → confidence -10, stake -20%. Flag explicitly in reasoning.
WARNING: If your edge DEPENDS on them being OUT, apply confidence -25 (not -10).
A Questionable player has ~50% chance of playing — never build a bet thesis on their absence.
Franchise player Day-To-Day → confidence -5, stake -10%.
If BOTH teams have franchise player uncertainty → evaluate net impact.

2. Franchise player availability: SECONDARY — a key absence can negate any NetRtg gap.
3. Home court: TERTIARY — worth 3-4pts in playoffs; decisive in close Game 7s.
4. Season NetRtg: CONTEXT ONLY at Game 7 stage; PRIMARY for Round 2 Game 1/2.
5. L15 NetRtg: directional only — weight less than in-series or home court.

→ Season NetRtg and current franchise player status are the primary inputs.

notes: ESPN live feed confirms four Round 2 series with updated scores: NYK leads PHI 3-0 (PHI facing elimination), DET leads 2-0 (opponent likely CLE — verify), OKC leads 2-0 (opponent verify), and SAS leads MIN 2-1 — the only truly competitive series with a clear variable (Edwards status). The LAL-HOU Game 7 remains the most analytically interesting pick, where Durant's confirmed absence/presence is the sole determinant of whether HOU has a legitimate case. Performance data shows ML bets are deeply negative (9W/13L, €-930) while spreads are profitable (14W/12L, €+274) and Medium confidence is significantly outperforming High confidence — this pattern suggests Scout should continue favouring spread markets and avoid high-stakes ML plays, particularly at the 1.70-1.89 odds range which shows the worst ROI (8W/10L, €-1023).

intelligence_gaps.gap: High confidence bets (17 bets, 35.3% WR, €-1099) are massively underperforming medium confidence (60% WR, €+958) — the confidence calibration appears systematically off, over-rating certainty.
intelligence_gaps.why: High confidence picks are losing at a rate suggesting the model is over-confident on its strongest signals; the EV calculation uses confidence as a key input and inflated confidence produces inflated EV on losing picks.
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: Consider tightening high-confidence threshold from 70-84 to 75-84 range, or adding a 'high confidence handicap' of -5 confidence for playoff picks where franchise player verification is roster-only rather than NBA official PDF confirmed.
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: Confidence 0.65 — 17 bets is a borderline sample for a structural confidence_staking change; want to see if this pattern holds across full 40-bet sample before patching the tier boundaries.

intelligence_gaps.gap: ML bets at 1.70-1.89 odds range are the worst performing segment (8W/10L, €-1023) — this is the range where most strong-favourite playoff picks cluster.
intelligence_gaps.why: At these odds, books already heavily price in the favourite — the market is efficient and our edge is thinnest, yet Scout may be drafting these based on NetRtg gaps that books have already incorporated.
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: Add a rule in market_rules: for ML picks in the 1.70-1.89 range, require NetRtg gap > 8pts OR in-series lead of 2+ games, not just the standard EV threshold; this would filter out marginal favourites where our edge is already priced out.
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: Confidence 0.68 — just below 0.70 threshold; the pattern is suggestive but playoff context (small sample of ~18 bets in this range) means this could be variance; flag for review after 5 more settled bets in this range.

intelligence_gaps.gap: OKC opponent in Round 2 West Semifinals is unclear — the four confirmed Round 2 participants are NYK, DET, MIN, OKC, but OKC's opponent is not explicitly identified in ESPN feed while SAS is separately listed in West Semifinals.
intelligence_gaps.why: If SAS is in West Semifinals vs OKC (Series B) and MIN is in West Semifinals vs [someone], there may be a bracket discrepancy — picking OKC or SAS without confirmed opponent creates an analytical blind spot for home court and in-series context.
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: ESPN bracket data needs explicit opponent mapping for each Round 2 series slot — the live feed should be queried with 'West Semifinals Series B: OKC vs [verify opponent]' before any OKC or remaining West team picks are drafted.
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: Infrastructure fix needed — this is a data pipeline clarity issue, not a rule gap; the current rules already require ESPN verification before drafting.

Performance data shows a clear and actionable pattern: Medium confidence (55-69) is profitable at 58.3% WR / +€739.80 while High confidence (70-84) is deeply underwater at 35.3% / -€1,099.81 — this is now 17 bets of evidence and the most important signal in the dataset. intelligence_gaps.gap: High-confidence picks (70-84 tier) are losing at 35.3% across 17 bets — far worse than Medium-confidence picks at 58.3%, suggesting the confidence calibration is systematically over-confident in that tier
intelligence_gaps.why: The staking rules assign 20-25% of bankroll to High confidence picks; if these are losing at 35.3%, the system is betting largest on its worst-performing tier, which explains the -€1,099.81 loss concentration
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: After 20 bets at High confidence, this warrants a strategic review of what signals are driving High confidence inflation — likely NetRtg-heavy analysis without sufficient in-series or injury weighting in playoff context; consider raising the High tier floor to 75 or adding a playoff-specific confidence cap
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: The rules file requires 20+ settled bets to patch confidence_staking; we are at 17 High-confidence bets which is borderline — holding for one more session to meet the formal evidence threshold before proposing a structural change

intelligence_gaps.gap: ML market is 40.9% win rate over 22 bets (-€930.10) while spreads are 53.8% over 26 bets (+€274.33) — a consistent and large divergence across the full sample
intelligence_gaps.why: Scout currently treats ML and spread as equal alternatives; if spreads are systematically outperforming ML by ~13 percentage points across a significant sample, the market_rules selectivity for ML should be raised or spreads should be default-preferred when both markets are available
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: Add a preference rule to market_rules: when both ML and spread show EV ≥ 0.05 on the same game, default to the spread market unless ML odds are ≥ 2.10 (where ML-specific value exists); also consider raising ML confidence floor from 50 to 55
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: Confidence is 0.72 that spreads outperform ML in this system, but the specific calibration change (raising ML floor to 55 vs 60 vs adding preference rule) has multiple valid implementations — patching market_rules language to add spread preference now with the floor change deferred to milestone review

intelligence_gaps.gap: OKC's Round 2 opponent identity is unconfirmed in current data — the skills file references an unknown opponent at 0-3 down
intelligence_gaps.why: Scout cannot evaluate OKC games without knowing the opponent's NetRtg, injury status, home court, and series context; a pick drafted with 'unknown opponent' risks using wrong franchise player rules or rest data
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: Mandatory ESPN bracket verification before any OKC pick is already flagged; no patch needed — infrastructure/data fetch issue, not a rules gap
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: Data pipeline issue — ESPN bracket lookup is required at Scout time; cannot resolve via rules patch

intelligence_gaps.suggestion: Add to franchise_player_rules a specific note: if Edwards status cannot be confirmed via NBA official PDF, apply confidence -20 to ALL MIN picks (not just -10) given he is the franchise player and was recently OUT; this is stronger than the standard GTD adjustment given the recent injury history

1. In-series result (2-3 games of data): PRIMARY signal — overrides season NetRtg.
2. Franchise player availability: SECONDARY — a key absence can negate any NetRtg gap.
3. Home court: TERTIARY — worth 3-4pts in playoffs; decisive in elimination games.
4. Season NetRtg: CONTEXT — useful for series where in-series data is thin (1-2 games).
5. L15 NetRtg: directional only — weight less than in-series or home court.

→ For Game 3/4 of Round 2: 2-3 games of in-series signal now available.

- OKC (+11.1) vs opponent: OKC leads 3-0 — gap too large for scheme to override (validated across 7 games total: 4-game Round 1 sweep + 3 games Round 2).
  Most reliable NetRtg predictor this postseason.

- LAL (+1.7) vs HOU (+5.4): LAL leads 4-2 despite inferior NetRtg — Durant OUT amplified LAL advantage.
  In-series is the only valid metric for Game 7.

- PHI (-0.2) vs BOS (+8.2): PHI won 4-3 — Tatum OUT negated 8.4pt BOS advantage completely.

Performance data shows a clear ML underperformance pattern (43.5% WR, -€725) versus spread outperformance (53.8% WR, +€274) — Scout should continue to prioritise spread evaluation over ML-default picks, particularly in series where NetRtg gaps are established and home court is clear. intelligence_gaps.gap: OKC's Round 2 opponent identity is unverified in the current data — ESPN feed shows 'Tied 2-2 (Game 5 next) [West Semifinals]' which conflicts with the OKC leads 3-0 entry and suggests a possible bracket mismatch in the live feed.
intelligence_gaps.why: Without confirmed opponent identity, any OKC pick cannot be properly evaluated for franchise player absences, NetRtg gaps, or home court assignment — a mismatch in the feed could cause Scout to draft a pick with wrong matchup assumptions.
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: Fetch ESPN bracket page directly before any OKC Round 2 pick — confirm opponent name, current series score, and next game venue. Do not draft OKC picks until opponent is confirmed by name.
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: Infrastructure verification needed — requires live ESPN bracket fetch; cannot resolve from current data alone without risking a wrong-team patch.

intelligence_gaps.gap: ML market is underperforming significantly (43.5% WR, -€725 across 23 bets) while spread bets are profitable (53.8% WR, +€274 across 26 bets) — the current market_rules confidence floor for ML (50) may be too low relative to spread floor (60), allowing too many marginal ML picks through.
intelligence_gaps.why: If ML picks at confidence 50-59 are driving the ML losses, raising the ML floor would filter out the worst-performing tier and redirect picks toward the more profitable spread market.
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: Analyse ML bets by confidence tier — if ML losses cluster at confidence 50-64 (medium-low tier), raise ML confidence floor from 50 to 60 to match spread floor. Confidence 0.72 that this is directionally correct based on market performance split.
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: Confidence is 0.72 — above threshold — but the performance stats do not break ML results down by confidence sub-tier, so the causal link between ML floor and losses cannot be confirmed precisely. Patching the floor without sub-tier evidence risks overcorrection. Flag for next milestone run with sub-tier breakdown.

intelligence_gaps.gap: High-confidence bets (85-100 tier) are severely underperforming: 7W/11L (38.9% WR, -€894.80) versus medium confidence bets at 58.3% WR (+€739.80) — the elite/high staking tiers may be over-sizing on picks that don't warrant it.
intelligence_gaps.why: This is the most important performance signal in the dataset: high-confidence picks are losing at a rate that destroys bankroll, while medium picks are profitable. If high-confidence sizing (20-30% bankroll) is applied to picks that prove wrong at 61% rate, the staking structure amplifies losses.
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: At 28 total bets, this pattern is emerging but not yet at the 20-bet-per-tier threshold for strategic patching. Flag for review at 35+ total bets. If pattern persists, reduce High tier staking from 20-25% to 15-20% and cap Elite at 20%.
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: Confidence 0.68 — just below 0.70 threshold. With only 18 high-confidence bets in sample, one or two results can shift the picture significantly; awaiting 25+ high-confidence bets before patching staking tiers.

playoff_patches.before:
L15 NetRtg CAVEAT FOR PLAYOFFS (Round 2 Semifinals — Games 5-6 Stage; WCF/ECF approaching)

CRITICAL HIERARCHY:
1. In-series result (4-5 games of data now available for all active series): PRIMARY signal — overrides season NetRtg.
2. Franchise player availability: SECONDARY — a key absence can negate any NetRtg gap.
3. Home court: TERTIARY — worth 3-4pts in playoffs; decisive in elimination/swing games.
4. Season NetRtg: CONTEXT — useful where in-series data is thin (new series).
5. L15 NetRtg: directional only — weight less than in-series or home court.

ROUND 2 SPECIAL RULE (updated to Game 5-6 stage):
→ For Game 5-6 of Round 2: 4-5 games of in-series signal now available for all active series.
→ Weight in-series results DECISIVELY ABOVE season NetRtg for ALL active series.
→ Home court (higher seed) is the decisive tiebreaker in close matchups (tied series especially).
→ Do NOT over-weight single-game swings — tactical adjustments happen every game.
→ EXCEPTION: If a team won Game 5 in an elimination context, that is a motivational data point, not just a tactical one.

Key NetRtg vs in-series divergences (updated):

- NYK (+6.5) vs PHI (-0.2): NYK swept PHI 4-0 — SERIES COMPLETE. 6.7pt gap validated completely.
  KEY LESSON: Large NetRtg gap + playoff motivation = sweep is possible even vs resilient opponents.

- DET (+8.2) vs CLE (+4.0): TIED 2-2 — in-series shows CLE is competitive despite 4.2pt gap.
  DET statistical edge is real but NOT overwhelming in practice. Home court for Game 5 is the PRIMARY edge.

- SAS (+8.3) vs MIN (+3.1): SAS leads 3-2 — MIN won Game 5 showing elimination urgency is real.
  5.2pt gap exists but MIN's survival to Game 6 suggests Edwards may be active (or MIN depth compensating).

L15 NetRtg CAVEAT FOR PLAYOFFS (Conference Finals — Games 1-2; New Series)

CRITICAL HIERARCHY:
1. In-series result: PRIMARY signal — but NO in-series data yet for ECF/WCF (series brand new).
   Once Game 1 result is available: immediately elevate to primary signal above all season stats.
2. Franchise player availability: SECONDARY — a key absence can negate any NetRtg gap.
3. Home court: TERTIARY — worth 3-4pts in playoffs; decisive in even matchups.
4. Season NetRtg: PRIMARY for Game 1 predictions (no in-series yet). Shifts to secondary after Game 1.
5. L15 NetRtg: directional only — weight less than home court or franchise player health.

CONFERENCE FINALS SPECIAL RULE:
→ For ECF/WCF Game 1: Season NetRtg is the PRIMARY signal (no in-series data available).
→ From Game 2 onwards: In-series momentum is decisive. Do not ignore a 1-0 series lead.
→ Rest gap matters most in Game 1: rested teams (NYK, OKC) have rust risk vs battle-tested teams (CLE, SAS).
→ Do NOT over-weight regular season H2H — Conference Finals opponents have elite coaching; they will adapt.

Active series NetRtg comparison:

ECF — NYK (+6.5) vs CLE (+4.0):
- NYK has +2.5pt advantage. Moderate edge — home court and franchise player health are the tiebreakers.
- CLE match-sharpness (7-game series) partially offsets NYK statistical edge in Game 1.
- Do NOT back CLE at odds shorter than 2.00 on the road in ECF Game 1 without clear Mitchell dominance signal.
- Minimum odds to bet NYK at home in ECF Game 1: only if EV ≥ 0.05 after rust adjustment (-5 conf).

WCF — OKC (+11.1) vs SAS (+8.3):
- OKC has +2.8pt edge. Both elite — this is the tightest NetRtg matchup left in the bracket.
- OKC's 8-game winning streak is a strong momentum signal; but extended rest = rust risk in Game 1.
- SAS match-sharpness (6-game series) + Wembanyama factor make SAS a live underdog in Game 1-2.
- Minimum odds to bet SAS in WCF: ≥ 1.85 (avoid backing SAS shorter given OKC's dominance signal).
- If Game 1 already played (Game 2 next per ESPN): use that result as primary signal IMMEDIATELY.

Round 2 effective NetRtg (final standings — all series complete):
- OKC: +11.1 (ADVANCED to WCF; Jalen Williams OUT — depth absorbed)
- SAS: +8.3 (ADVANCED to WCF; Wembanyama active — verify MANDATORY)
- DET: +8.2 (ELIMINATED — CLE won 4-3)
- NYK: +6.5 (ADVANCED to ECF; Brunson/KAT — verify active)
- CLE: +4.0 (ADVANCED to ECF; Mitchell — verify active; won 7-game series showing real playoff toughness)
- MIN: +3.1 (ELIMINATED — SAS won 4-2)
- LAL: +1.7 (Active Round 2; Doncic OUT — in-series execution carries)
- PHI: ELIMINATED (swept 0-4 by NYK)
- HOU: ELIMINATED (lost Game 7 to LAL)

KEY LESSONS FOR CONFERENCE FINALS:
1. OKC +11.1 has been the most reliable signal all postseason (8-0 record). WCF opponents face same gap.
2. CLE proved a 4.2pt NetRtg deficit does NOT equal easy series — DET couldn't close it out in 7 games.
3. Battle-tested teams (CLE 7 games, SAS 6 games) enter Conference Finals sharper than rested sweepers (NYK, OKC).
4. Franchise player absence remains the single biggest override: Tatum (BOS), Durant (HOU), Doncic (LAL) all decisive.
5. For any Conference Finals pick: MANDATORY franchise player health check before drafting.

The performance data (57 bets) reveals a critical structural pattern: High-confidence picks are losing at 38.9% (-€895) while Medium-confidence picks win at 56.8% (+€632), suggesting our confidence calibration is systematically overconfident in the 70-84 range — today's patches add extra scrutiny criteria before staking 20%+ on any pick.
